perturbation.original_text: HOVIONE shall invoice INTERSECT upon dispatch of the API. INTERSECT shall pay the price to HOVIONE for API within thirty (30) calendar days of the date of invoice of such API. Payments shall be made to HOVIONE by wire transfer.
perturbation.changed_text: HOVIONE shall invoice INTERSECT upon dispatch of the API. INTERSECT shall pay the fee to HOVIONE for API within sixty (60) calendar days of the date of invoice of such API. Payments shall be made to HOVIONE by wire transfer.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'price' is changed to 'fee' and payment days are being extended from 'thirty' to 'sixty' days. This creates an in-text contradiction because the document now contains two different payment schedules. This affects payment enforcement since the contract has conflicting payment terms. In the original text, 'price' and 'fee' are used to reffer to the same term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HOVIONE shall invoice INTERSECT upon dispatch of the API. INTERSECT shall pay the fee to HOVIONE for API within sixty (60) calendar days of the date of invoice of such API. Payments shall be made to HOVIONE by wire transfer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the payment period from 30 to 60 days. The contradicted text also states a payment period of 60 days. Because the contradicted text has been updated to reflect the change, there is no contradiction in payment terms between the two sections.

perturbation.original_text: HOVIONE represents and warrants to INTERSECT that: (a) It has full right and power to enter into this Agreement and perform its obligations hereunder in accordance with its terms; (b) The API and all components and ingredients thereof shall be manufactured and delivered in strict compliance with: (i) the Product Specifications; (ii) the methods processes and procedures, including the site manufacture, set forth in the DMF, together with all applicable regulatory requirements relating to the manufacture of the API 4 (c) the plant(s) for manufacture of the API is and shall be in compliance with all applicable cGMPs and that such plant(s) is and shall continue to be available for inspection if and when the Regulatory Authorities so requests;
perturbation.changed_text: HOVIONE represents and warrants to INTERSECT that: (a) It has full right and power to enter into this Agreement and perform its obligations hereunder in accordance with its terms; (b) The API and all components and ingredients thereof shall be manufactured and delivered in strict compliance with: (i) the Product Specifications; (ii) the methods processes and procedures, including the site manufacture, set forth in the DMF, together with all applicable regulatory requirements relating to the manufacture of the compounds 4 (c) the plant(s) for manufacture of the API is and shall be in compliance with all applicable cGMPs and that such plant(s) is and shall continue to be available for inspection if and when the Regulatory Authorities so requests;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'API' is changed to 'compounds'. This change creates an in-text contradiction, especially when considering HOVIONE's warranty that the plant(s) is in compliance with cGMPs for 'API.' Now it is ambigous if 'compounds' is another term for 'API', opening HOVIONE to manufacturing 'compounds' that do not meet the Product Specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HOVIONE represents and warrants to INTERSECT that: (a) It has full right and power to enter into this Agreement and perform its obligations hereunder in accordance with its terms; (b) The API and all components and ingredients thereof shall be manufactured and delivered in strict compliance with: (i) the Product Specifications; (ii) the methods processes and procedures, including the site manufacture, set forth in the DMF, together with all applicable regulatory requirements relating to the manufacture of the compounds 4 (c) the plant(s) for manufacture of the API is and shall be in compliance with all applicable cGMPs and that such plant(s) is and shall continue to be available for inspection if and when the Regulatory Authorities so requests;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'API' with 'compounds' in a warranty clause. The contradicted text is actually the same as the changed text in this instance, however the explanation clarifies that this change creates an ambiguity regarding whether 'compounds' is interchangeable with 'API'. Hovione warrants that the plant is in compliance with cGMPs for API, but the change raises the possibility that 'compounds' manufactured there might not meet product specifications. This creates a direct conflict, as the plant's compliance with cGMPs for API no longer guarantees that the 'compounds' will meet the required specifications, thus, violating HOVIONE's warranty.

perturbation.original_text: 14.1. Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any interest herein may be assigned, in whole or in part, by either party without the prior written consent of the other, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed, except that either party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement: (a) to an affiliate, division or subsidiary of such party; and/or (b) to any third party that acquires all or substantially all of the stock or assets of such party, whether by asset sale, stock sale, merger or otherwise, and, in any such event such assignee shall assume the transferring party's obligations hereunder. However, notwithstanding any such assignment, in the case of an assignment to an affiliate, division or subsidiary, the transferring party shall remain liable under this Agreement (in addition to the transferee) unless such liability is specifically waived in writing by the other party hereto. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto, and their respective successors and permitted assigns. (a) Buyout. In the case that either company is acquired by, or merges with, another company which has reason to not wish to continue the relationship, that company may make a contract buyout payment [*] for the [*], with a [*] buyout payment amount of [*].
perturbation.changed_text: 14.1. Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any interest herein may be assigned, in whole or in part, by either party without the prior written consent of the other, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed, except that either party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement: (a) to an affiliate, division or subsidiary of such party; and/or (b) to any third party that acquires all or substantially all of the stock or assets of such party, whether by asset sale, stock sale, merger or otherwise, and, in any such event such assignee shall assume the transferring party's obligations hereunder. However, notwithstanding any such assignment, in the case of an assignment to an affiliate, division or subsidiary, the transferring party shall remain liable under this Agreement (in addition to the transferee) unless such liability is specifically waived in writing by the other party hereto. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto, and their respective successors and permitted assigns. (a) Termination Fee. In the case that either company is acquired by, or merges with, another company which has reason to not wish to continue the relationship, that company may make a contract buyout payment [*] for the [*], with a [*] buyout payment amount of [*].
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Buyout' is changed to 'Termination Fee'. Although the process remains the same, the use of two different terms to refer to the same concept within the same section creates confusion. The original text has two different terms, which leads to a contradictory understanding of the assignment and the buyout option. Since the contract has now two different methods to assign the rights, it creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.1. Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any interest herein may be assigned, in whole or in part, by either party without the prior written consent of the other, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed, except that either party may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement: (a) to an affiliate, division or subsidiary of such party; and/or (b) to any third party that acquires all or substantially all of the stock or assets of such party, whether by asset sale, stock sale, merger or otherwise, and, in any such event such assignee shall assume the transferring party's obligations hereunder. However, notwithstanding any such assignment, in the case of an assignment to an affiliate, division or subsidiary, the transferring party shall remain liable under this Agreement (in addition to the transferee) unless such liability is specifically waived in writing by the other party hereto. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto, and their respective successors and permitted assigns. (a) Termination Fee. In the case that either company is acquired by, or merges with, another company which has reason to not wish to continue the relationship, that company may make a contract buyout payment [*] for the [*], with a [*] buyout payment amount of [*].
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Buyout' to 'Termination Fee' in subsection (a) creates a contradiction because the contradicted text references both 'Termination Fee' and 'contract buyout payment,' implying they are distinct concepts when they should be synonymous within the same clause. This violates the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction,' where the use of multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations and uncertainty regarding the method for assigning rights. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.
